SymbolOrHook: HouseRegion.OnLocationChanged and HouseRegion.OnMoveInto vendor notice path
CurrentBehavior: HouseRegion sends the vendor relocation NoticeGump only when internalized vendors exist, the mobile is inside, the previous location was outside, the mobile is the owner, the mobile is alive, and the NoticeGump is not already open; OnMoveInto also has current null/deleted mobile and house guards before house movement/access checks.
PolicyQuestion: Should the owner-only house customization notice or house movement checks change as a region/map policy risk?
DesignDecision: Keep current house-region policy. The owner-only vendor reclaim notice is an intentional housing workflow and current source already has narrow ownership, liveness, inside/outside, duplicate-gump, null, deleted, and base movement guards. No region behavior change is approved.
SourceEvidence: Data/Scripts/System/Regions/HouseRegion.cs:L256-L274 sends the owner vendor notice from OnLocationChanged; Data/Scripts/System/Regions/HouseRegion.cs:L277-L288 guards OnMoveInto before movement policy; Data/Scripts/System/Regions/HouseRegion.cs:L358-L376 repeats the owner-only vendor notice on successful move-in; POST-BATCH-K PBK-2261 queued this because line-only source evidence was insufficient.
AffectedSurface: Housing; house movement and access policy; owner vendor relocation notice after customization.
SourceChangeNeed: None. No C# source edit is needed for this policy decision.
VerificationNeeded: Docs-only policy verification; future source edits must run targeted region/hook scan, Server.csproj Debug/x86 build, and compile-only runtime verification.
RollbackNotes: Reopen PBN-0793/RB-05141 and queue a narrow source follow-up before changing HouseRegion movement, access, or owner notice behavior.
Reason: Policy design resolved the row as intentional guarded housing behavior, not a current source defect.

CurrentBehavior: OnEnter returns for null or deleted mobiles, calls base.OnEnter, logs player entries only when the mobile is PlayerMobile, and calls RegionMusic.MusicRegion. RegionMusic itself only acts when the mobile is PlayerMobile and has explicit PirateRegion music handling.
PolicyQuestion: Should PirateRegion.OnEnter add new map or player-only policy restrictions?
DesignDecision: Keep current PirateRegion entry policy. Region membership scopes the map context, player logging and music are already player-gated by current source/helper code, and no travel/PvP/housing behavior change is justified.
SourceEvidence: Data/Scripts/System/Regions/PirateRegion.cs:L26-L37 has null/deleted guard, base.OnEnter, PlayerMobile logging, and RegionMusic call; Data/Scripts/System/Regions/RegionMusic.cs:L12-L15 gates music behavior to PlayerMobile; Data/Scripts/System/Regions/RegionMusic.cs:L211-L214 has PirateRegion music handling; POST-BATCH-K PBK-2276 queued this region override for design review.
AffectedSurface: PirateRegion entry; player region logs; player region music.
SourceChangeNeed: None. No C# source edit is needed for this policy decision.
VerificationNeeded: Docs-only policy verification; future source edits must run targeted region/hook scan, Server.csproj Debug/x86 build, and compile-only runtime verification.

Reason: Policy design resolved the row as intentional PirateRegion entry behavior with current null/deleted and player-only helper guards.

CurrentBehavior: OnExit returns for null or deleted mobiles, calls base.OnExit, teleports named pirate/undead BaseCreature types back to Home with effects and sound, and logs other exits through LoggingFunctions. LoggingFunctions itself only writes region logs for PlayerMobile.
PolicyQuestion: Should PirateRegion.OnExit change pirate containment, logging, or map policy?
DesignDecision: Keep current PirateRegion exit policy. Named pirate/undead creature containment is intentional region behavior, non-contained exits are routed through player-only logging, and current source already has null/deleted guards. No behavior change is approved.
